frontend.overlap_assessment: 70-95% content overlap on anti-patterns, typography rules, motion discipline, library discipline, WCAG targets. The 'Not for X' clauses added recently help at the margin but do not substantively separate the skills — users invoking 'design a UI' have no clear basis to pick among 5 nearly-identical rulesets. frontend-excellence-standards and frontend-design-excellence are near-duplicates. ultrathink-frontend could be a mode flag inside frontend-pe. frontend-uiux-designer is justifiable only as the constellation-team role.
frontend.action: Collapse to 2 skills: frontend-pe (absorbing ultrathink-frontend ULTRATHINK mode + frontend-excellence-standards content) and frontend-design-excellence (keep standalone). Optionally retain frontend-uiux-designer strictly as a constellation-role doc.

backend.overlap_assessment: Three language-agnostic skills (backend-pe + backend-architecture-standards + backend-system-design-expert) overlap ~80% — identical decision tables for API style, consistency, caching, messaging, reliability. backend-pe is the only one doing routing; the other two duplicate its cross-cutting content. Seven language variants are LEAN (99-133 lines) and surface language-specific failure modes (GIL, event-loop lag, UB/RAII, virtual threads, tsconfig strictness) that a generic skill cannot capture.
backend.action: Merge backend-architecture-standards and backend-system-design-expert into backend-pe/references/architecture-patterns.md (or retire). Keep all 7 language variants — the specialization is genuinely valuable. Optional: fold backend-pe-javascript into backend-pe-typescript with a plain-JS mode flag (low priority).
backend.opinion: Language variants ARE valuable — do not consolidate them. The redundancy is in the three language-agnostic 'architecture' skills, not in the language specializations.

learning.overlap_assessment: techtutor and gabriel-petersson-topdown-mentor overlap on triggers and audience (senior engineers rebuilding DSA/system-design intuition, mandatory visualizations, intuition-before-formalism). Stylistic differences (6-layer framework vs. 5-step recursive loop with 5 response modes) are real but shallow. lecture-alchemist is a transcript-to-notes PIPELINE, not a conversational tutor — distinct. professor-alex-interview is specific to FAANG/HFT prep with anti-gaming safeguards + quant content — distinct.
learning.action: Consider merging techtutor and gabriel-petersson-topdown-mentor into one mentor skill with two modes (layered-explanation vs. recursive-gap-filling). Defer to user on whether the two distinct 'voices' are worth keeping. Keep lecture-alchemist and professor-alex-interview.

orchestrators.overlap_assessment: kingmode and super-mode-core are near-duplicates — same 3 modes (Default/ULTRATHINK/KINGMODE), same 4-step workflow (clarify/decide/implement/validate), same anti-hallucination rules, same output formats per mode. Only unique piece of super-mode-core is 'load backend/frontend/security standards on demand' — that is ~10 lines of content. constellation-team operates on a different axis (role-based delivery: PM -> PE -> BE -> FE -> QA -> DevOps) and is genuinely complementary.
orchestrators.action: Merge super-mode-core into kingmode (absorb the domain-standards-loading section). Keep constellation-team.
